to Install OpenClaw Step by Step\",\"description\":\"A complete step-by-step guide to installing OpenClaw on your system. 3:["$","span",null,{"className":"mx-2","children":"/"}]
4:["$","$L2",null,{"href":"/tutorials","className":"capitalize hover:text-zinc-900 dark:hover:text-white","children":"tutorials"}]
5:["$","span",null,{"className":"mx-2","children":"/"}]
6:["$","span",null,{"className":"text-zinc-900 dark:text-white truncate","children":"How to Install OpenClaw Step by Step"}]
c:T1953,<h2>Introduction</h2>
<p>This guide will walk you through the complete installation process for OpenClaw. Whether you're using Windows, macOS, or Linux, we've got you covered.</p>
<h2>Prerequisites</h2>
<p>Before installing OpenClaw, ensure you have:</p>
<ul>
<li><strong>Node.js</strong> version 18 or higher</li>
<li><strong>npm</strong> or <strong>pnpm</strong> or <strong>yarn</strong> package manager</li>
<li>A code editor (VS Code recommended)</li>
<li>Git (optional but recommended)</li>
</ul>
<h3>Checking Prerequisites</h3>
<p>Open your terminal and run:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">node --version
npm --version
</code></pre>
<p>If you don't have Node.js installed, download it from <a href="https://nodejs.org/">nodejs.org</a>.</p>
<h2>Installation Methods</h2>
<h3>Method 1: Using npm (Recommended)</h3>
<p>The easiest way to install OpenClaw is using npm:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">npm install -g openclaw
</code></pre>
<p>Verify the installation:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">openclaw --version
</code></pre>
<h3>Method 2: Using pnpm</h3>
<p>If you prefer pnpm:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">pnpm add -g openclaw
</code></pre>
<h3>Method 3: Using Docker</h3>
<p>For containerized environments:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">docker pull openclaw/openclaw:latest
</code></pre>
<h3>Method 4: From Source</h3>
<p>For developers who want to contribute or customize:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">git clone https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw.git
cd openclaw
npm install
npm run build
npm link
</code></pre>
<h2>Platform-Specific Instructions</h2>
<h3>Windows</h3>
<ol>
<li>Open PowerShell or Command Prompt as Administrator</li>
<li>Run the npm installation command</li>
<li>Add OpenClaw to your PATH if needed:
<pre><code class="language-powershell">setx PATH "%PATH%;%APPDATA%\npm"
</code></pre>
</li>
<li>Restart your terminal</li>
</ol>
<h3>macOS</h3>
<ol>
<li>Open Terminal</li>
<li>If you don't have Homebrew, install it:
<pre><code class="language-bash">/bin/bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Homebrew/install/HEAD/install.sh)"
</code></pre>
</li>
<li>Install Node.js via Homebrew:
<pre><code class="language-bash">brew install node
</code></pre>
</li>
<li>Install OpenClaw:
<pre><code class="language-bash">npm install -g openclaw
</code></pre>
</li>
</ol>
<h3>Linux</h3>
<h4>Ubuntu/Debian</h4>
<pre><code class="language-bash"># Update package list
sudo apt update

# Install Node.js
sudo apt install nodejs npm

# Install OpenClaw
sudo npm install -g openclaw
</code></pre>
<h4>CentOS/RHEL/Fedora</h4>
<pre><code class="language-bash"># Install Node.js
sudo dnf install nodejs npm

# Install OpenClaw
sudo npm install -g openclaw
</code></pre>
<h4>Arch Linux</h4>
<pre><code class="language-bash"># Install Node.js
sudo pacman -S nodejs npm

# Install OpenClaw
sudo npm install -g openclaw
</code></pre>
<h2>Initial Configuration</h2>
<p>After installation, configure OpenClaw:</p>
<ol>
<li>
<p><strong>Initialize the configuration</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">openclaw init
</code></pre>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Set up your API keys</strong> (if using cloud AI providers):</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">openclaw config set openai.api_key YOUR_API_KEY
</code></pre>
</li>
<li>
<p><strong>Verify configuration</strong>:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">openclaw config list
</code></pre>
</li>
</ol>
<h2>Creating Your First Project</h2>
<p>Let's create a simple project to verify everything is working:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash"># Create a new project
openclaw new my-first-agent
cd my-first-agent

# Start the development server
openclaw dev
</code></pre>
<p>You should see output indicating the server is running on <code>http://localhost:3000</code>.</p>
<h2>Common Issues and Solutions</h2>
<h3>Permission Denied (macOS/Linux)</h3>
<p>If you get permission errors, you may need to fix npm permissions:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">mkdir ~/.npm-global
npm config set prefix '~/.npm-global'
echo 'export PATH=~/.npm-global/bin:$PATH' >> ~/.bashrc
source ~/.bashrc
</code></pre>
<h3>Node Version Too Old</h3>
<p>If you see a Node version error, upgrade Node.js:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash"># Using nvm (recommended)
nvm install 18
nvm use 18

# Or download from nodejs.org
</code></pre>
<h3>Port Already in Use</h3>
<p>If port 3000 is taken, specify a different port:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">openclaw dev --port 3001
</code></pre>
<h3>Windows Defender or Antivirus Warnings</h3>
<p>Some antivirus software may flag OpenClaw. Add an exception for:</p>
<ul>
<li>The OpenClaw installation directory</li>
<li>The <code>openclaw</code> executable</li>
</ul>
<h2>Next Steps</h2>
<p>Now that OpenClaw is installed, you're ready to:</p>
<ol>
<li><strong>Build your first agent</strong>: Follow our <a href="/posts/build-first-ai-agent">beginner tutorial</a></li>
<li><strong>Explore templates</strong>: Check out the template gallery</li>
<li><strong>Read the docs</strong>: Dive deeper into OpenClaw's features</li>
<li><strong>Join the community</strong>: Connect with other users</li>
</ol>
<h2>Updating OpenClaw</h2>
<p>To update to the latest version:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">npm update -g openclaw
</code></pre>
<p>Or reinstall:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">npm uninstall -g openclaw
npm install -g openclaw
</code></pre>
<h2>Uninstalling OpenClaw</h2>
<p>If you need to remove OpenClaw:</p>
<pre><code class="language-bash">npm uninstall -g openclaw
</code></pre>
<h2>FAQ</h2>
<p><strong>Q: Can I install OpenClaw without admin/sudo access?</strong></p>
<p>A: Yes, use a Node version manager like nvm or install locally in your project.</p>
<p><strong>Q: How much disk space does OpenClaw need?</strong></p>
<p>A: Approximately 500MB for the base installation, plus space for your projects.</p>
<p><strong>Q: Can I use OpenClaw offline?</strong></p>
<p>A: Yes, if you're using local AI models. Cloud providers require internet access.</p>
<p><strong>Q: Is there a GUI version?</strong></p>
<p>A: OpenClaw is primarily CLI-based, but web interfaces are available through community plugins.</p>
<h2>Conclusion</h2>
<p>You now have OpenClaw installed and ready to go! The installation process is straightforward across all platforms. If you encounter any issues not covered here, check our <a href="/posts/troubleshooting">troubleshooting guide</a> or reach out to the community.</p>
<p>Happy building!</p>
